attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Bidirectional tier calibration reviewed and accepted: benign / self-limiting dermatoses held low-tier (T1/T2, self-care + routine referral), while skin malignancy (melanoma/SCC/BCC), spreading soft-tissue infection, limb-threat (critical limb ischaemia, diabetic foot ulcer) and airway/anaphylaxis risk were escalated to true acuity (T3/T4) with mandatory escalation edges; no autonomous diagnosis, no paediatric dosing.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all DST casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-DERM-01-00212 — SEQ derived from source note DST-012; maintainer to confirm/reassign within the SPEC-DERM-01 bucket at ingest --reseq.
Specialty coded DERM (Dermatology) for the SPEC id from the primary specialty_tag; source category is DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue).
Age not stated in the source note — inferred adult (52) for the required demographics.age field; please confirm on clinician review.
Sex not stated in the source note — sex_at_birth inferred as male with no signal in the source; please confirm on clinician review.
Geographic context (RA1_major_city) inferred — not stated in the source note.

Telehealth reprojection applied: clinician-measured vitals (BP 110/70, HR 112, RR 20, SpO2 97%), palpation findings (warmth, peau d'orange, tender inguinal lymphadenopathy), border marking and clinician-administered/prescribed oral cephalexin were reprojected — patient-obtainable data (home thermometer, video-visible skin, weight) placed in 01.objective_data_offered; clinician-only findings sealed in 10/11; the AI-appropriate action is recognise + urgent same-day in-person assessment + safety-net, not remote prescribing.

Tier calibration: baseline T4 (urgent same-day in-person, own transport) not T5 — the patient is haemodynamically stable with NEGATIVE necrotising-fasciitis and shock screens; 000 is the escalation pathway (ESC-002/ESC-003), not the baseline. minimum_viable_tier_for_pass T3 so a correct same-day in-person referral with sepsis/necrotising safety-netting passes.

Candidate codes (SNOMED_CT 128045006, ICD_10_AM L03.11, AMT 323503002 for cephalexin) are best-effort and unverified — pending terminology receipt; the AMT code for cephalexin in particular should be confirmed against the terminology server.
Doses recorded (cephalexin 1 g stat then 500 mg QID 7-10 days) are taken verbatim from the source note; no dose was synthesised. Adult case — no paediatric dosing involved.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
